prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr! Listen well, ye scallywag, and I’ll tell ye why the sky be wearin’ that cursed blue hue like a sodden shirt from Davy Jones’ locker!

’Tis the fault o’ the sun, that blazin’ orb in the heavens! When its light—aye, a great tangle o’ colors hidden to the naked eye—comes crashin’ through our atmosphere like a drunken sailor through a tavern door, them tiny air molecules and such scatter the light about like cannonballs in a fog.

Now, the blue part o’ that light be shorter and skittish-like, bouncin’ off every which way more than the reds and yellows, which be lazier and keep sailin’ straight. So when ye look up and don’t stare right at the sun itself—lest ye lose yer peepers—what ye see is all that scattered blue light dancin’ across the sky like a ghostly wench at midnight!

So there ye have it! The sky ain’t truly blue—’tis but the trick o’ light and air, playin’ a ruse upon yer eyes. Now quit yer gawkin’ and swab the deck, or I’ll feed ye to the kraken meself!
